# http_client

> Ada 2022 HTTP/HTTPS client library with explicit configuration, conservative
> security defaults, deterministic offline tests, and documented stable versus
> experimental package boundaries.

## Identity

- Alire crate: `httpclient`
- Ada root package: `Http_Client`
- Main project: `httpclient.gpr`
- Primary high-level API: `Http_Client.Clients`
- Public package map: `docs/PUBLIC_PACKAGES.md`
- Stable API contract: `docs/STABLE_API_CONTRACT.md`
- AI usage guide: `docs/AI_USAGE_GUIDE.md`
- Agent notes: `AGENTS.md`

## What It Provides

Stable user-facing areas include URI parsing, headers, requests, responses,
status values, HTTP/1.1 execution, HTTPS/TLS configuration, high-level clients,
redirects, retries, cookies, decompression, streaming, uploads, multipart,
proxies, SOCKS5, authentication helpers, diagnostics, caches, bounded async,
HTTP/2 configuration/execution support, and explicit protocol-discovery helpers.

HTTP/3 and QUIC packages are experimental boundaries. Production HTTP/3 request
execution requires an explicitly configured supported QUIC backend; otherwise the
library must fail with deterministic unsupported statuses or fall back only where
configured before unsafe request transmission.

## First Packages To Use

```ada
with Http_Client.Clients;
with Http_Client.Errors;
```

For lower-level request construction:

```ada
with Http_Client.Headers;
with Http_Client.Requests;
with Http_Client.Responses;
with Http_Client.Types;
with Http_Client.URI;
```

For optional behavior, prefer the documented configuration packages such as
`Http_Client.Retry`, `Http_Client.Proxies`, `Http_Client.Proxies.SOCKS`,
`Http_Client.Diagnostics`, `Http_Client.Cache`, `Http_Client.Cache.Persistent`,
`Http_Client.Decompression`, and `Http_Client.Protocol_Discovery`.

## Defaults To Preserve

- TLS certificate and hostname verification are enabled by default.
- Redirects, retries, cookies, decompression, caches, proxies, SOCKS, diagnostics,
  async execution, protocol discovery, and HTTP/3 are disabled until configured.
- HTTPS-to-HTTP downgrade redirects are blocked by default.
- Cross-origin credentials are stripped on redirects by default.
- Diagnostics redact secrets by default.
- Public-internet access is not required for the default test suite.
- The library must not silently read browser profiles, OS credential stores,
  password managers, or host proxy settings.

## Validation

Toolchain rule: every active manifest pins `gnat_native = "=15.2.1"`. Do not
run plain system GNAT/GPR tools; use `alr exec -- ...` and require
`alr exec -- gnatls --version` to report `GNATLS 15.x`.

Typical local validation:

```sh
alr exec -- gnatls --version
alr exec -- gprbuild -P httpclient.gpr
alr exec -- gprbuild -P examples/examples.gpr
alr exec -- gprbuild -P tests/tests.gpr
./tests/bin/tests
alr exec -- gprbuild -P tests/api_stability/api_stability.gpr
```

Release-surface and corpus validation:

```sh
alr exec -- gprbuild -P tools/tools.gpr
./tools/bin/check_release_surface
./tools/bin/check_aunit_suite
./tools/bin/check_security_corpus
./tools/bin/check_git_smart_http_release
```

Optional coverage gate when GNAT/gcov tooling is available:

```sh
cd tests && alr exec -- ../tools/bin/run_aunit_coverage
```

## Documentation Map

Start with `README.md`, then `docs/DOCUMENTATION_INDEX.md`. Key focused docs:

- `docs/QUICKSTART.md`: first build/test and first request
- `docs/EXAMPLES.md`: compile-checked examples
- `docs/CONFIGURATION.md`: option model and defaults
- `docs/SECURITY_MODEL.md`: security boundaries and non-goals
- `docs/DEFAULT_LIMITS.md`: documented release limits
- `docs/THREADING_AND_OWNERSHIP.md`: ownership and task-safety
- `docs/STATUS_MODEL.md`: result-status model
- `docs/HTTP2_GUIDE.md`: HTTP/2 guidance
- `docs/HTTP3_EXPERIMENTAL.md`: HTTP/3/QUIC boundaries
- `docs/RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md`: final release gate list

## Non-Goals

This is not a browser networking stack. Do not add implicit PAC/WPAD/browser
proxy discovery, browser profile import, browser cache integration, service
workers, automatic login/OAuth/OIDC/SAML flows, NTLM/Negotiate/Kerberos, OS
credential stores, password-manager integration, SOCKS UDP/BIND, MASQUE,
CONNECT-UDP, WebTransport, Tor control behavior, or hidden browser-like policy
unless a future explicit compatibility review adds such scope.
